name: AidTrace | RastroAyuda
db_description:
## ¿Qué construiste?

Construí **AidTrace | RastroAyuda**, una app offline-first compatible para desktop, mobile y Telegram para rastrear ayuda humanitaria con QR y pruebas en Celo blockchain

## ¿Para quién es?

Para equipos humanitarios, refugios, donantes y auditores que necesitan verificar entregas de agua, comida, medicinas o kits de emergencia.

## ¿Cómo ayuda?

Permite registrar entregas, recepciones y revisiones incluso sin internet. Cuando vuelve la conexión, guarda la prueba en Celo y muestra el enlace en Celoscan.

Los usuarios en campo no necesitan wallet, CELO ni saber de blockchain.

## ¿Qué deberían probar primero?

Crear un lote, generar el QR, registrar una entrega desde el móvil y revisar el historial con el link a Celoscan.

También pueden probar Telegram con:

`CELO1 entregar 13 cajas de ibuprofeno para el refugio mayor`


linkedin.com/in/luis-alejandro

analysis.summary: AidTrace / RastroAyuda is an offline-first humanitarian supply-tracking PWA built on Celo Mainnet. Coordinators create QR-labeled aid batches; field workers record custody handoffs (pickup, delivery, damage) via browser or Telegram. Events queue locally in localStorage and IndexedDB when offline, then sync automatically through a serverless relay that writes verifiable proofs to a deployed Solidity smart contract. The contract (AidTraceLedger) is genuinely deployed and verified on Celo Mainnet. This is real, functional code — not boilerplate — with thoughtful offline-resilience design tailored to disaster connectivity conditions.
analysis.project_type: Offline-first PWA + serverless API + Celo smart contract (blockchain-backed aid traceability)

backend: Express v5 (listed as dependency but API routes are plain functions)
database: No traditional DB — Celo blockchain events are source of truth

architecture.pattern: Offline-first PWA + serverless relay + blockchain event ledger
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: No ORM. Blockchain events (viem getLogs / writeContract) are the persistence layer. Supabase and Redis are used exclusively for distributed locking of the single relayer wallet to avoid nonce collisions on Celo.
architecture.api_design: Two Vercel serverless functions: POST /api/zavu handles both browser relay packets (schema: aidtrace.relay.v1) and Zavu Telegram webhooks (type: message.inbound); GET /api/timeline reads AidTraceLedger logs from Celo via block range chunking. No REST framework — raw req/res.
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Clean: app.js handles UI/state/sync, sw.js handles offline cache/background sync, api/ handles server-side blockchain writes and reads, AidTraceLedger.sol is the on-chain ledger. Each layer has one clear responsibility.
architecture.modularity: Flat directory structure with 6-7 source files. Small enough that modularity is not a concern; no unnecessary abstraction.
notable_patterns: Offline-first with localStorage queue + IndexedDB in service worker
Distributed write-lock (Supabase RPC or Redis SETNX) to serialize relayer wallet transactions and avoid nonce collisions
Schema-versioned on-chain events via bytes32 actionType + dataHash + referenceURI — extensible without redeployment
Bilateral command parser for Spanish/English Telegram messages with alias normalization (CELO1 -> AT-CELO-1)
QR codes generated client-side with printable PDF via window.print()
sendBeacon used for best-effort relay on pagehide

code_organization.directory_structure: Flat root-level structure with index.html, app.js, styles.css, sw.js, and separate api/ and zavu/ folders. No framework scaffold. Simple and navigable but not scalable.
code_organization.naming_quality: Consistent and descriptive: batchId, actionType, relayPacket, enqueueCeloWrite, acquireDistributedLock. Constants are uppercase. Functions named for what they do.
code_organization.documentation_quality: README is thorough: explains architecture, Telegram command syntax, live contract address, test path, and developer summary. Inline comments are present but minimal. No JSDoc.
code_organization.reasoning: The code is clearly hand-crafted, not AI-generated boilerplate — every function has real logic. Small surface area means the flat structure works fine. Zero tests and no CI are the main gaps.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: Private key (RASTROAYUDA_RELAYER_PRIVATE_KEY) and API keys are read from environment variables; nothing hardcoded. Wallet addresses are hardcoded in app.js but are public keys, not secrets.
production_readiness.security_notes: No authentication or rate limiting on /api/zavu — any party who discovers the endpoint can submit events to the contract (though gas is paid by the relayer). CORS is set to wildcard (*). No input length limits beyond publicAuditText truncating to 120 chars.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Timeline API fetches all blocks from contract deployment in 4900-block chunks, re-fetching all historical logs on every request — this will become slow/expensive as block count grows. Relayer wallet is a single key; the distributed lock serializes all writes which limits throughput. Blocking parallel block fetches with Promise.all for individual block timestamps adds latency.
production_readiness.reasoning: The app is genuinely deployed and functional on Celo Mainnet with real transactions. Environment config is correct and secrets are externalized. The main production gaps are: no relay endpoint auth/rate limiting, the timeline block-scan approach won't scale, and there is no deploy config (vercel.json) visible in the repo.

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: Real — the smart contract is deployed and verified at 0xaf5c40e82ac9255479a1f447e81992b71c4f4934 on Celo Mainnet; the app is live at aidtrace-rastroayuda.vercel.app; 49 commits show iterative development of actual features.
maturity.reasoning: 49 commits from one contributor over 2 days show rapid iteration on real functionality. No tests of any kind exist, and there is no CI pipeline. The Telegram command parser with bilingual alias normalization and the distributed locking logic are non-trivial implementations that demonstrate genuine effort.

viability.reasoning: This is one of the stronger entries: the smart contract is already live on mainnet, the PWA is deployed and working, Telegram integration is real, and the offline-first design directly addresses the connectivity reality of disaster zones. The no-wallet / no-fee design for field users removes a major adoption barrier. The main production gap (timeline block scan scaling) is fixable. Could be put in front of real aid coordinators in Venezuela today with minimal additional work.

key_features: Offline-first PWA: records queue to localStorage and IndexedDB when offline, auto-sync on reconnection
QR label generation and printable PDF for each aid batch
Custody event recording (created, pickup, arrived, delivered, damaged, review)
Blockchain-backed proofs on Celo Mainnet via AidTraceLedger smart contract (already deployed)
Telegram bot integration for low-connectivity field operators (no wallet or browser required)
Bilingual UI (Spanish/English) with full i18n via translation keys
Distributed write-lock (Supabase Postgres or Upstash Redis) to prevent nonce collisions on shared relayer wallet
Timeline view with paginated history pulled from on-chain events
Service worker background sync for deferred relay when browser regains connectivity
analysis.merge_potential: Medium-high. The blockchain traceability layer is a unique contribution that complements shelter-mapping or people-finder projects. The Telegram relay and offline-first queue pattern could be re-used as a messaging primitive by other projects in the hackathon.
red_flags: No authentication or rate limiting on /api/zavu relay endpoint — any caller can submit events at the relayer's expense
Timeline block scan fetches all historical logs on every API call — will grow unbounded and slow as usage accumulates
Single relayer private key with no rotation strategy; compromise requires redeploying with a new submitter key
No tests of any kind (unit, integration, or contract tests)
manifest.webmanifest has empty icons array — PWA installability is incomplete

evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: Humanitarian aid logistics in disaster zones suffer from two compounding failures: no connectivity and no accountability. Supplies get lost, stolen, or unverified. AidTrace directly attacks both: offline-first queuing means field workers record deliveries even without internet, and every event is eventually anchored to a deployed Celo Mainnet smart contract for tamper-proof audit trails. The Telegram bot removes the need for a browser, wallet, or crypto knowledge — matching the real capabilities of on-the-ground aid workers. This is precisely the accountability gap that exists in Venezuela earthquake response today.
evaluation.live_demo_notes: The live app at aidtrace-rastroayuda.vercel.app loads as a fully interactive PWA — not a landing page. Users can create aid batches, select supply type/quantity/origin/destination, generate printable QR labels, and record custody handoff events (pickup, arrival, delivery, damage). The app displays "Offline ready" status and includes a bilingual toggle. The Celo Mainnet smart contract (AidTraceLedger at 0xaf5c40e82ac9255479a1f447e81992b71c4f4934) is live and verified. A demo video is also available. This is a genuinely working product a coordinator could use today.

diffusion.recommended_angle: Frame around the zero-barrier field worker experience: no wallet, no crypto knowledge, no internet required — just Telegram or a browser. Every box of medicine, every water delivery, provably recorded on blockchain. Lead with the Telegram command demo ('CELO1 entregar 13 cajas de ibuprofeno') as the human hook, then show the Celoscan proof link as the accountability payoff. Bilingual angle reinforces real-world deployment readiness.
gaps: screenshots of the QR label and Celoscan proof view to make social posts visual
branding assets (logo, OG image — PWA manifest has empty icons)
evidence of real users or pilot deployment
a short explainer GIF or screen recording clip for X/Twitter

evaluation.one_line_pitch: AidTrace rastrea cada entrega humanitaria en blockchain — funciona sin internet, sin wallet, solo con Telegram — para que ninguna caja de ayuda se pierda sin prueba.
